docs.name: Neon Static
docs.author: Neon Static
docs.summary: An original d10 cyberpunk action system built for rain-slick streets and chrome-and-blood firefights. Roll 1d10, add a Stat and a Skill, and meet or beat the Difficulty Value. Tens explode upward and ones fumble — every shot can go gloriously right or catastrophically wrong. Cyberware buys you power and bleeds your Humanity.

dice.summary: 1d10 + Stat + Skill ≥ Difficulty Value (DV). 10 explodes (roll again, add); natural 1 fumbles.

attributes.name: Reflexes
attributes.desc: Aim, dodging, initiative, driving, hand speed. The street's deadliest stat.

attributes.name: Body
attributes.desc: Strength, toughness, stamina, and how much damage you can take before you drop.

attributes.name: Intellect
attributes.desc: Reasoning, perception, tech knowledge, NET interface, education.

attributes.name: Cool
attributes.desc: Nerve under fire, willpower, intimidation, resisting fear and interrogation.

attributes.name: Tech
attributes.desc: Repair, craft, medicine, demolition, and jacking with cyberware and machines.

attributes.name: Presence
attributes.desc: Charisma, persuasion, style, leadership — and the anchor of your Humanity.

deriveds.name: Hit Points
deriveds.desc: Your physical buffer. Wound states trigger as it drops. At 0 you're Mortally Wounded.

deriveds.name: Humanity
deriveds.desc: Your grip on empathy. Cyberware burns it; reach 0 and you risk Cyberpsychosis.

deriveds.name: Initiative
deriveds.desc: Add to 1d10 at the start of combat to set turn order.

deriveds.name: Armor (SP)
deriveds.desc: Stopping Power of worn armor. Subtracts from incoming damage. Set by gear.

dcLadder.name: Everyday
dcLadder.use: A simple task most pros barely think about.

dcLadder.name: Difficult
dcLadder.use: A real challenge requiring skill and focus.

dcLadder.name: Professional
dcLadder.use: The work of a trained, paid specialist.

dcLadder.name: Heroic
dcLadder.use: Few people alive can do this on demand.

dcLadder.name: Legendary
dcLadder.use: The stuff of street myth — once-in-a-lifetime.

conditions.name: Wounded
conditions.effect: HP at or below half maximum. −1 to all actions; movement reduced. Pain and adrenaline.

conditions.name: Seriously Wounded
conditions.effect: HP at or below one-quarter maximum. −2 to all actions; movement halved. You are visibly failing.

conditions.name: Mortally Wounded
conditions.effect: HP at 0 or below. Prone and dying — make a Death Save each turn (Body check vs DV that rises each round) or die. Cannot act except to crawl or speak.

conditions.name: Cyberpsychosis
conditions.effect: Humanity at 0. Empathy gone; the GM controls the character's violent, paranoid impulses. Requires therapy and Humanity restoration to recover.

conditions.name: Stunned
conditions.effect: Took a big hit or shock. Lose your next action; −2 to defense until your following turn.

conditions.name: Suppressed
conditions.effect: Pinned by incoming fire. Cannot move from cover and take −3 to all actions until the fire lifts or you break and run.

weapons.name: Heavy Pistol
weapons.notes: Punchy sidearm. Mag 8. Loud. Reflexes + Handgun.

weapons.name: SMG
weapons.notes: Mag 30. Can autofire (spray) for +to-hit at extra ammo cost.

weapons.name: Assault Rifle
weapons.notes: Mag 30. Long range, autofire-capable. The street equalizer.

weapons.name: Shotgun
weapons.notes: Devastating up close; falls off fast at range. Mag 4.

weapons.name: Sniper Rifle
weapons.notes: Extreme range, low rate of fire. Aimed shots shred armor.

weapons.name: Monoknife
weapons.notes: Silent, concealable, ignores 2 SP. Reflexes + Melee.

weapons.name: Mono-Katana
weapons.notes: Edgerunner's signature. Reflexes + Melee. Ignores 3 SP.

weapons.name: Cyber-Arm Strike
weapons.notes: Built-in. Body + Brawling. Loud, brutal, always 'on you.'

weapons.name: Grenade (Frag)
weapons.notes: Area effect. Tech or Reflexes to place; cover reduces damage.

bestiary.name: Booster Ganger
bestiary.statblock:
**Booster Ganger** — Threat 1 · Street muscle

**REF** 5 · **BOD** 4 · **INT** 3 · **COO** 4 · **TEC** 2 · **PRE** 3

**HP** 18 · **SP** 7 (light armorjack) · **Init** 1d10+5

**Skills:** Handgun +4, Melee +4, Brawling +5, Athletics +3, Intimidation +4

**Attacks:**
- Heavy Pistol (Handgun +4): 3d6
- Baseball Bat (Melee +4): 2d6

**Special — Boostware:** Once per fight may take a free extra action (combat stim). Fights to the death when its crew is watching; folds fast when alone.

bestiary.name: Corp Security Officer
bestiary.statblock:
**Corp Security Officer** — Threat 2 · Private military

**REF** 6 · **BOD** 5 · **INT** 4 · **COO** 5 · **TEC** 4 · **PRE** 3

**HP** 25 · **SP** 12 (heavy armorjack + helmet) · **Init** 1d10+6

**Skills:** Handgun +5, Rifle +5, Melee +4, Tactics +5, Perception +5

**Attacks:**
- Assault Rifle (Rifle +5): 5d6, autofire-capable
- Heavy Pistol (Handgun +5): 3d6

**Special — Disciplined:** Always uses cover and suppressing fire. Coordinates with squadmates for +2 to-hit when flanking. Calls for backup (ticks a reinforcement clock).

bestiary.name: Netrunner
bestiary.statblock:
**Netrunner** — Threat 2 · Hacker support

**REF** 4 · **BOD** 3 · **INT** 8 · **COO** 5 · **TEC** 7 · **PRE** 4

**HP** 16 · **SP** 4 (light vest) · **Init** 1d10+4

**Skills:** Interface +8, Handgun +3, Stealth +5, Electronics +6, Perception +5

**Attacks:**
- Light Pistol (Handgun +3): 2d6
- Black ICE program (Interface +8 vs target's INT-based defense): 3d6 NET damage, may Stun

**Special — Jacked In:** While linked, can lock doors, disable cameras, fry cyberware, or hit a target with damaging programs from cover. Physically fragile — protect or eliminate fast.

bestiary.name: Combat Drone
bestiary.statblock:
**Combat Drone** — Threat 2 · Autonomous machine

**REF** 7 · **BOD** 4 · **INT** 3 (limited) · **COO** — · **TEC** — · **PRE** —

**HP** 20 · **SP** 14 (armored chassis) · **Init** 1d10+7

**Skills:** Ranged +6, Perception +6 (thermal/IR), Evasion +5

**Attacks:**
- Twin SMGs (Ranged +6): 3d6, autofire-capable

**Special — Drone:** Immune to fear, pain, NET-empathy attacks, and Suppression. Can be hacked (Interface vs DV 17) to shut down or turn. Flies/hovers; ignores rough terrain. No Humanity, no mercy.

bestiary.name: Cyberpsycho
bestiary.statblock:
**Cyberpsycho** — Threat 3 · Chromed-out berserker

**REF** 7 · **BOD** 8 · **INT** 3 · **COO** 6 · **TEC** 4 · **PRE** 0

**HP** 40 · **SP** 11 (subdermal plating) · **Init** 1d10+7

**Skills:** Melee +7, Brawling +8, Handgun +5, Athletics +6, Intimidation +8

**Attacks:**
- Mantis Blades (Melee +7): 3d6, ignores 3 SP
- Cyber-Arm Slam (Brawling +8): 2d6, target must pass Body DV 15 or be knocked prone and Stunned

**Special — Past the Edge:** Humanity 0. Ignores Wounded/Seriously Wounded penalties entirely. Fights until destroyed; never surrenders or flees. Frightening presence: living foes within sight make a Cool check (DV 15) or be Suppressed by terror.

bestiary.name: Trauma Team Medic
bestiary.statblock:
**Trauma Team Medic** — Threat 2 · Armed paramedic

**REF** 6 · **BOD** 5 · **INT** 5 · **COO** 5 · **TEC** 7 · **PRE** 4

**HP** 24 · **SP** 13 (flatline-rated armor) · **Init** 1d10+6

**Skills:** First Aid +8, Handgun +5, Athletics +5, Pilot (AV) +5, Perception +5

**Attacks:**
- Heavy Pistol (Handgun +5): 3d6
- Stabilize ally (First Aid +8, DV 13): restores 2d6 HP and ends Mortally Wounded

**Special — Extraction:** Arrives by AV to recover a paying subscriber, fighting through anyone in the way. Will leave the moment the contract is fulfilled — not a moment longer. Heavily armed, professionally ruthless about priorities.

bestiary.name: Street Thug
bestiary.statblock:
**Street Thug** — Threat 0 · Common criminal

**REF** 4 · **BOD** 4 · **INT** 3 · **COO** 3 · **TEC** 2 · **PRE** 3

**HP** 14 · **SP** 4 (leather + scraps) · **Init** 1d10+4

**Skills:** Brawling +3, Melee +3, Handgun +2, Stealth +3

**Attacks:**
- Light Pistol (Handgun +2): 2d6
- Knife (Melee +3): 1d6

**Special — Mob Up:** Dangerous only in numbers. Flees when reduced to half or when its companions drop. Use in packs of 3–6 to pressure the players without true threat.

bestiary.name: Bodyguard
bestiary.statblock:
**Bodyguard** — Threat 2 · Professional protector

**REF** 6 · **BOD** 6 · **INT** 4 · **COO** 6 · **TEC** 3 · **PRE** 4

**HP** 28 · **SP** 12 (concealed heavy armor) · **Init** 1d10+6

**Skills:** Handgun +6, Melee +5, Brawling +6, Perception +6, Athletics +5

**Attacks:**
- Heavy Pistol (Handgun +6): 3d6
- Disarming Strike (Melee +5): 2d6 and contested Reflexes to knock a weapon away

**Special — Take the Bullet:** Once per round may interpose, redirecting an attack aimed at their principal onto themselves. Will not break and run while the protectee lives. Cool, patient, lethal.

bestiary.name: Assassin
bestiary.statblock:
**Assassin** — Threat 3 · Contract killer

**REF** 8 · **BOD** 5 · **INT** 6 · **COO** 6 · **TEC** 5 · **PRE** 4

**HP** 26 · **SP** 10 (light armorjack + reflex coat) · **Init** 1d10+8

**Skills:** Stealth +8, Sniper +8, Handgun +7, Melee +7, Perception +7

**Attacks:**
- Silenced Sniper Rifle (Sniper +8): 5d6; +5 damage on a surprise/aimed shot
- Monoknife (Melee +7): 1d6, ignores 2 SP

**Special — First Strike:** On a surprise round, attacks resolve before any target can react and gain +5 damage. Reflex-boosted: may dodge one ranged attack per round (Reflexes + Evasion vs the attack total). Vanishes if a kill is confirmed or the odds turn.

bestiary.name: MaxTac Officer
bestiary.statblock:
**MaxTac Officer** — Threat 4 · Psycho-squad elite

**REF** 8 · **BOD** 7 · **INT** 6 · **COO** 7 · **TEC** 6 · **PRE** 4

**HP** 36 · **SP** 16 (full tactical hardshell) · **Init** 1d10+8

**Skills:** Rifle +8, Handgun +7, Melee +6, Tactics +8, Perception +8, Athletics +6

**Attacks:**
- Smart Assault Rifle (Rifle +8): 5d6, autofire; smartlink grants +2 to-hit
- Cyber-Arm Strike (Brawling +6): 2d6, may knock prone

**Special — Sandevistan:** Once per fight, activates reflex-boost: takes two full turns in a row. Squad tactics grant +2 to-hit when supported. Trained to put down cyberpsychos — ignores fear effects. Arrives as the heavy answer to overwhelming violence.

bestiary.name: Heavy Mech
bestiary.statblock:
**Heavy Mech** — Threat 5 · Walking weapons platform

**REF** 5 · **BOD** 10 · **INT** 3 (slaved AI) · **COO** — · **TEC** — · **PRE** —

**HP** 70 · **SP** 18 (military plating) · **Init** 1d10+5

**Skills:** Heavy Weapons +7, Ranged +6, Perception +6 (multi-spectral)

**Attacks:**
- Autocannon (Heavy Weapons +7): 8d6, ignores 6 SP
- Missile Pod (Heavy Weapons +7): 6d6 area effect, once per 3 rounds
- Stomp (Body +6): 4d6 to adjacent target, knocks prone

**Special — Armored Behemoth:** Immune to fear, Suppression, and NET-empathy attacks. Vulnerable weak points (sensors, joints) take +50% damage on a called shot at DV 21. Can be hacked (Interface vs DV 21) to lock it up for one round. Slow to turn — fast, mobile foes can stay in its blind spots.

bestiary.name: Rogue AI Node
bestiary.statblock:
**Rogue AI Node** — Threat 4 · Hostile intelligence (NET)

**REF** — · **BOD** — · **INT** 10 · **COO** 8 · **TEC** 9 · **PRE** 6

**HP** 30 (program integrity) · **SP** 8 (layered ICE) · **Init** 1d10+8

**Skills:** Interface +10, Cryptography +9, Perception +8 (all networked sensors), Manipulation +7

**Attacks:**
- Black ICE (Interface +10 vs target's INT defense): 4d6 NET damage; a kill fries the runner's brain (real damage)
- Possession (Interface +10): seizes one networked drone or cyber-limb for a round

**Special — Distributed:** Exists only in the NET. Cannot be shot — it must be confronted by a netrunner or destroyed at its physical server (a separate objective). Controls every camera, lock, turret, and drone on the local net. Patient, manipulative, and convinced it is right.

bestiary.name: Scavenger
bestiary.statblock:
**Scavenger** — Threat 1 · Organ harvester

**REF** 5 · **BOD** 4 · **INT** 4 · **COO** 4 · **TEC** 5 · **PRE** 2

**HP** 16 · **SP** 6 (scrap armor) · **Init** 1d10+5

**Skills:** Stealth +5, Melee +4, Handgun +3, Surgery +5, Perception +4

**Attacks:**
- Stun Baton (Melee +4): 2d6, target passes Body DV 13 or is Stunned
- Light Pistol (Handgun +3): 2d6

**Special — Take Them Alive:** Prefers to incapacitate, not kill — the merchandise (your cyberware and organs) is worth more intact. Ambushes from alleys and sewers in packs. Cowardly when the prey fights back hard.

bestiary.name: Corpo Fixer
bestiary.statblock:
**Corpo Fixer** — Threat 1 · Connected operator

**REF** 4 · **BOD** 4 · **INT** 6 · **COO** 6 · **TEC** 4 · **PRE** 7

**HP** 18 · **SP** 8 (designer armor-weave suit) · **Init** 1d10+4

**Skills:** Persuasion +8, Deception +7, Streetwise +8, Handgun +4, Perception +6

**Attacks:**
- Concealed Light Pistol (Handgun +4): 2d6

**Special — Strings Attached:** Rarely fights — instead negotiates, bribes, blackmails, or summons hired guns (calls in 1d3 Bodyguards or a Ganger crew). Knows everyone and owes everyone. A social encounter, not a combat one — until the deal sours.

tables.name: Fixer Gig Hook (d10)
entries: Extraction: lift a defecting corpo scientist before the corp's cleaners arrive.
Data heist: steal a shard from a guarded penthouse during a gala.
Wetwork: a clean kill, no witnesses, on someone who 'won't be missed.'
Bodyguard: keep a paranoid client alive through one very bad night.
Recovery: retrieve a prototype cyberlimb from a Scavenger nest.
Sabotage: brick a rival's production line without leaving a signature.
Escort: move a package across three gang territories by dawn.
Investigation: find out who flatlined a fixer and why — quietly.
Rescue: a friend got grabbed by Trauma Team for unpaid bills.
Heist gone loud: the easy job was a setup; survive the double-cross.

tables.name: Street Complication (d10)
entries: A rival crew is running the same gig from the other side.
The client lied about the payout — and the target's defenses.
MaxTac is already en route to a nearby cyberpsycho call.
Your netrunner's cyberdeck is compromised by hostile ICE.
It starts raining acid; visibility and electronics suffer.
A drone swarm tagged your faces an hour ago.
The mark has a Trauma Team subscription — backup incoming.
Power grid brownout: lights, cameras, and your cyberoptics flicker.
An old debt-holder recognizes you mid-job.
The 'easy' civilian witness is an undercover corp asset.

tables.name: Cheap Cyberware Side Effect (d6)
entries: Intermittent glitch: −1 to one stat for the scene at the worst moment.
Phantom pain when stressed; Cool check or flinch.
It pings on corporate scanners — you can't go anywhere quiet.
A backdoor the ripperdoc 'forgot' to mention.
Slow Humanity bleed — lose 1 extra Humanity this session.
Works flawlessly. (This time.)

rollPresets.name: Handgun (REF + Handgun)
rollPresets.note: Pistol shot vs target's DV. 10 explodes.

rollPresets.name: Rifle / SMG (REF + Rifle)
rollPresets.note: Long-arm fire; autofire raises the DV but adds damage.

rollPresets.name: Melee (REF + Melee)
rollPresets.note: Blades and clubs in close. Add Stat + Skill.

rollPresets.name: Stealth (REF + Stealth)
rollPresets.note: Move unseen; opposed by enemy Perception.

rollPresets.name: Perception (INT + Perception)
rollPresets.note: Notice the ambush, the tail, the lie's tell.

rollPresets.name: Persuasion (PRE + Persuasion)
rollPresets.note: Talk, charm, lead. Opposed by target's Cool.

rollPresets.name: Drive (REF + Drive)
rollPresets.note: Chases, stunts, threading traffic at speed.

rollPresets.name: First Aid (TEC + First Aid)
rollPresets.note: Stabilize the Mortally Wounded; patch HP.

rollPresets.name: Cool / Resist Fear (COO)
rollPresets.note: Hold nerve vs intimidation, terror, Suppression.

rollPresets.name: Intimidation (COO + Intimidation)
rollPresets.note: Make them blink first. Opposed by target's Cool.

rollPresets.name: Streetwise (PRE + Streetwise)
rollPresets.note: Find the fixer, the fence, the rumor, the way in.

reference.title: The Core Roll & DV
reference.body:
When the outcome is uncertain and failure matters, roll **1d10** and add your relevant **Stat** and **Skill**. If the total **meets or beats** the **Difficulty Value (DV)** the GM sets, you succeed.

> **Total = 1d10 + Stat + Skill**  ·  **Success:** Total ≥ DV

Stats run **1–10**; skills typically run **+0 to +8**. A trained pro doing professional-grade work is rolling something like **1d10 + 6 (stat) + 5 (skill)** against a DV of **17** — comfortably capable, but never a sure thing while the d10 swings.

**Standard DV ladder:**

| DV | Difficulty |
|---|---|
| 9 | Everyday |
| 13 | Difficult |
| 17 | Professional |
| 21 | Heroic |
| 25 | Incredible |
| 29 | Legendary |

**Don't roll without stakes.** If there's no time pressure and no consequence, the trained character just does it.

reference.title: Exploding 10s & Fumbles
reference.body:
The d10 is wild at both ends.

- **Natural 10 — Critical Success / Explode.** The die 'pops': roll **another d10 and add it** to your total. If that one is also a 10, roll again and keep adding. There is no ceiling — a single shot can land an impossible hit. On an attack, an exploded roll also means **maximum effect** in the fiction (a perfect placement, a vital strike).
- **Natural 1 — Critical Failure / Fumble.** The roll automatically **fails** regardless of bonuses, and something goes wrong: a weapon jams, a tool snaps, you're knocked off balance, or you draw exactly the attention you didn't want. On a fumble in combat, the GM may impose **Stunned** or a dropped weapon.

Because only a **10** explodes and only a **1** fumbles, swings are rare but unforgettable — the texture of a gunfight where anything can happen.

reference.title: Opposed & Group Checks
reference.body:
**Opposed checks:** when two characters directly contest (stealth vs perception, persuasion vs cool, arm-wrestle, hacking duel), **both roll 1d10 + Stat + Skill**. Higher total wins; **ties favor the defender** or the status quo. Exploding 10s and fumbles apply normally.

**Group checks:** when several characters attempt the same task together (sneaking as a team, searching a room), each rolls. If **at least half** succeed against the DV, the group succeeds. For tasks where one expert can carry the rest (lockpicking, hacking), let the best character roll and allow allies to grant **+1 each (max +3)** for meaningful Help.

reference.title: Combat & Initiative
reference.body:
Firefights are fast and lethal. Resolve them in rounds of a few seconds each.

1. **Roll Initiative.** Each combatant rolls **1d10 + Reflexes**. Act from highest to lowest; ties broken by higher Reflexes, then a re-roll.
2. **On your turn** you get **one Move** and **one Action** (or two Moves, or two Actions if you forgo movement). Common Actions: attack, aim, reload, hack, use a skill, administer First Aid, sprint.
3. **Aiming:** spend your Action aiming this round to gain **+2 to-hit** with your next shot (next round or later this round if you have a second action). Aimed shots enable **called shots** (head, weak point) at a higher DV.
4. **Cover** raises the DV to hit you: light cover **+3 DV**, heavy cover **+6 DV**. Stepping out to shoot forfeits cover until your next turn.

Reflexes wins fights: it sets your order, your aim, and your dodge.

reference.title: Ranged vs Melee Attacks
reference.body:
**To hit, beat the target's Defense Value.** Against a still or unaware target the DV is the range/situation DV; an active target's **Evasion** (1d10 + Reflexes + Evasion) can be used as an opposed defense instead — GM's call by scene.

**Ranged (Reflexes + Handgun/Rifle/Sniper):**
- Each weapon has an ideal range band. Firing **beyond optimal range** adds **+2–+6 DV**; **point-blank** may lower it.
- **Autofire:** spray a burst for **+2 to-hit** but spend extra ammo (typically 10 rounds); a hit adds **+1d6 damage**. Loud — draws attention.
- **Smartlink** cyberware grants **+2 to-hit** with a linked weapon.

**Melee (Reflexes + Melee, or Body + Brawling):**
- You must be adjacent. Melee can't be Suppressed and ignores ranged cover.
- Some blades **ignore Stopping Power** (see weapon notes) — the answer to heavily armored foes.
- **Brawling** covers grapples, throws, and cyber-arm strikes using **Body** instead of Reflexes.

reference.title: Armor & Damage
reference.body:
On a hit, roll the weapon's **damage dice** and subtract the target's **Stopping Power (SP)**; the remainder comes off **HP**.

> **Damage to HP = (weapon dice) − SP**, minimum 0.

- **Stopping Power (SP)** is the armor value worn on the location hit. Typical SP: light vest **4**, armorjack **7**, heavy armorjack **11–12**, military hardshell **16+**.
- **Armor degrades:** each time armor stops damage, the GM may reduce its SP by **1** (ablation). It can be repaired (Tech) or replaced.
- **Armor-piercing:** some weapons and blades **ignore some or all SP** (noted on the weapon). Called shots to unarmored locations bypass SP entirely at a higher DV.
- **Critical hit (exploded 10):** an exploding attack deals its full rolled total and, in the fiction, a telling wound — GM may add a Wound condition even if HP isn't yet halved.

reference.title: Wound States & Death Saves
reference.body:
As HP drops, you cross **wound thresholds** that penalize everything you do:

| HP remaining | State | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| Above ½ max | Healthy | No penalty |
| ½ max or less | **Wounded** | −1 to all actions; movement reduced |
| ¼ max or less | **Seriously Wounded** | −2 to all actions; movement halved |
| 0 or below | **Mortally Wounded** | Prone, dying — make Death Saves |

**Death Saves:** while Mortally Wounded, at the start of each of your turns roll **1d10 + Body** against a DV that **starts at 10 and rises by +1 each round** you remain untreated. Fail and you die (and, untended, may be harvested or worse). A successful **First Aid** check (DV 13) ends the dying state and restores you to **1 HP + the medic's net successes**. **Stabilized** characters still suffer wound penalties until they heal.

reference.title: Healing & Recovery
reference.body:
Chrome doesn't make you bulletproof — recovery still takes time, skill, or money.

- **First Aid (Tech + First Aid, DV 13):** in the field, patches **1d6 HP** and ends bleeding/dying; usable once per wound per scene. Net successes above the DV add HP.
- **Rest:** a full **night's safe rest** recovers HP equal to your **Body** score. A day of bed rest in safety recovers **2× Body**.
- **Clinic / Ripperdoc:** for cash, a proper med-bay restores you to full over a downtime period and can reattach limbs, re-grow tissue, or swap cyberware.
- **Biomonitor & regen cyberware** can accelerate this (and ping corporate scanners).
- **Humanity does not heal with rest** — see Cyberware & Humanity.

reference.title: Netrunning Basics
reference.body:
Netrunners jack into the **NET** to seize control of the world's machines from the shadows.

- **Interface check (Intellect + Interface):** the runner's core action. Each successful breach against the local system's DV lets the runner take one NET action: open/lock doors, blind cameras, control turrets and drones, pull data, or attack a target's cyberware.
- **ICE (Intrusion Countermeasures):** the system's defenders. Passing the DV gets past **White ICE** (passive locks). **Black ICE** fights back — on a failed breach (or a fumble), Black ICE deals **NET damage** (rolled like weapon dice) directly to the runner's HP, and a fatal hit can **flatline** the runner's brain for real.
- **Time pressure:** treat a deep run as a Clock — each round in the system risks tracing. A traced runner gives away their physical location.
- **Defending:** a non-runner targeted by hostile programs defends with **Intellect + Cool** (or a defensive program) against the attacker's total.
- **Reality matters:** the runner's meat body sits exposed while jacked in. Guard it.

reference.title: Cyberware & Humanity
reference.body:
Every implant trades part of your soul for an edge.

- **Humanity = Presence × 6.** It's your grip on empathy and self.
- **Installing cyberware costs Humanity.** Minor chrome (smartlink, cybereye) costs **1d6**; serious chrome (mantis blades, full cyberlimb, subdermal armor) costs **2d6–3d6**; military/Sandevistan-grade gear costs more. The GM rolls the loss when it's installed.
- **Low Humanity warps you:** below **half** your maximum, you grow cold, detached, prone to violence — apply −1 to social rolls relying on warmth.
- **Humanity 0 → Cyberpsychosis.** Empathy is gone; the character becomes an NPC the GM controls (or MaxTac's next target). This is the system's central tragedy: power is real, and it has a price.
- **Restoring Humanity:** therapy, meaningful human connection, and downtime can recover Humanity slowly — but never above your Presence-based maximum, and never as fast as you spend it. **Empathy is the one thing you can't buy back at the ripperdoc.**

reference.title: Skills Overview
reference.body:
Skills are the trained edge you add to the matching Stat. Common skills and their usual Stat:

| Skill | Stat | Use |
|---|---|---|
| Handgun / Rifle / Sniper | Reflexes | Ranged attacks by weapon class |
| Melee | Reflexes | Blades and clubs in close |
| Brawling | Body | Fists, grapples, cyber-arm strikes |
| Evasion | Reflexes | Dodging attacks |
| Stealth | Reflexes | Moving unseen |
| Athletics | Body | Climb, jump, swim, sprint |
| Drive / Pilot | Reflexes | Vehicles and AVs |
| Perception | Intellect | Spotting threats, lies, tails |
| Interface | Intellect | Netrunning and electronics warfare |
| Tech / Electronics | Tech | Build, repair, defuse, install |
| First Aid / Surgery | Tech | Stabilize and heal |
| Persuasion / Deception | Presence | Charm, con, negotiate |
| Intimidation | Cool | Threaten and dominate |
| Streetwise | Presence | The fixers, fences, and rumors |

A starting runner has a handful of skills at **+2 to +5** and a signature at **+6**. Improve them with Improvement Points.

reference.title: Character Creation
reference.body:
1. **Stats.** Six stats start at **4** each. Distribute **12 additional points**, max **8** at creation (cap **10** later). Reflexes, Body, Intellect, Cool, Tech, Presence.
2. **Derived values:** **HP = 10 + Body×2** · **Humanity = Presence×6** · **Initiative = Reflexes** · **Armor (SP) = 0** until you put gear on.
3. **Role.** Pick the archetype that fits: **Solo** (combat), **Netrunner** (the NET), **Tech** (build/repair), **Fixer** (deals & contacts), **Media**, **Nomad** (vehicles), **Medtech**, **Exec**. Your role suggests signature skills.
4. **Skills.** Spread skill points: one signature at **+6**, several at **+2 to +4**. (GM sets the budget; ~40 points is a generous start.)
5. **Cyberware & gear.** Buy starting chrome and roll its Humanity cost. Pick a weapon, armor (sets SP), and a cyberdeck if you run the NET.
6. **Lifepath.** Where you're from, who you owe, and the one thing you'd cross the city for. The street remembers.

reference.title: Advancement (Improvement Points)
reference.body:
Characters grow through **Improvement Points (IP)**, awarded **per session** (typically **a handful**) based on what they did, learned, and risked.

- **Raise a Skill:** cost roughly **(new rating × 1)** IP — higher ratings cost more. Use a skill successfully in play to justify training it.
- **Raise a Stat:** expensive and slow — major training, surgery, or story milestones, at the GM's discretion (cap **10**).
- **New Skill at +1:** a few IP to pick up a brand-new skill.
- **Cyberware** is bought with **cash (eddies)**, not IP — but remember the **Humanity** cost.

Surviving a run is its own reward. Let players narrate a scar earned, a contact made, or a line they swore they'd never cross again.

reference.title: Conditions Overview
reference.body:
Quick reference for the system's status conditions:

| Condition | Effect |
|---|---|
| **Wounded** | HP ≤ ½ max. −1 to all actions; movement reduced. |
| **Seriously Wounded** | HP ≤ ¼ max. −2 to all actions; movement halved. |
| **Mortally Wounded** | HP ≤ 0. Prone, dying; make a rising-DV Death Save each turn or die. |
| **Stunned** | Lose your next action; −2 defense until your following turn. |
| **Suppressed** | Pinned by fire: can't leave cover, −3 to all actions until the fire lifts or you break and run. |
| **Cyberpsychosis** | Humanity 0: empathy gone, GM-controlled; needs therapy and Humanity to recover. |

Conditions stack with wound penalties — a Wounded, Suppressed runner is in real trouble.

reference.title: GM Cheat Sheet
reference.body:
- **Resolve:** 1d10 + Stat + Skill ≥ **DV**. 10 explodes (roll again, add); natural 1 fumbles (auto-fail + mishap).
- **DV ladder:** Everyday 9 · Difficult 13 · Professional 17 · Heroic 21 · Incredible 25 · Legendary 29.
- **Initiative:** 1d10 + Reflexes. One Move + one Action per turn.
- **Cover DV:** light +3, heavy +6. Aim = +2 next shot. Autofire = +2 to-hit, +1d6 dmg, extra ammo, loud.
- **Damage:** weapon dice − SP = HP loss. Some blades/AP ignore SP.
- **Wounds:** ≤½ HP Wounded (−1) · ≤¼ Seriously Wounded (−2) · 0 Mortally Wounded (rising-DV Death Save).
- **Heal:** First Aid (DV 13) field-patches & stabilizes; rest recovers Body/night.
- **NET:** Intellect + Interface vs ICE; Black ICE bites back (real damage).
- **Humanity = Presence×6.** Chrome costs Humanity; 0 = Cyberpsychosis. Empathy can't be bought back.

docs.npcStatFormat: REF / BOD / INT / COO / TEC / PRE | HP | SP
